data.problem_definition: Users need a reliable, accessible, and easy-to-use tool to perform basic and advanced mathematical calculations efficiently through a web browser.

user_personas.name: Alice, The Everyday User
user_personas.needs: Quick basic calculations (addition, subtraction, multiplication, division), clear interface, easy to use on any device.
user_personas.frustrations: Complex interfaces, slow loading times, lack of accessibility.

user_personas.name: Bob, The STEM Professional
user_personas.needs: Scientific functions (trigonometry, logarithms, exponents), memory functions, calculation history, precision.
user_personas.frustrations: Missing advanced functions, inconvenient input methods, loss of previous calculations.

advanced: Scientific Functions (sin, cos, tan, log, ln, sqrt, power)
enhancement: Responsive design for various screen sizes
Keyboard support for input and operations
Error handling and display (e.g., 'Divide by zero')

phase_2: Scientific Functions (sin, cos, tan, log, ln, sqrt, power)
phase_3: Advanced scientific functions (e.g., factorials, inverse trig)
Accessibility enhancements (full ARIA support, high contrast)

use_cases.title: Basic Calculation
use_cases.goal: Quickly calculate the sum of two numbers.
use_cases.scenario: Alice needs to add up expenses for a grocery trip.

use_cases.title: Scientific Calculation
use_cases.goal: Calculate the sine of an angle.
use_cases.scenario: Bob needs to solve a trigonometry problem for his project.

use_cases.title: Correcting Input
use_cases.goal: Correct a wrong number entered without clearing the entire calculation.
use_cases.scenario: Alice typed '1235' instead of '1234' and wants to delete the '5'.

user_flow: User Flow: Performing a basic calculation (2 + 3)

out_of_scope: Backend database for user accounts or saving calculations persistently across sessions (beyond local storage)
